key_insight: PhyNex is an LLM-driven autonomous agent that iteratively searches for effective computational physics methods while enforcing physical constraints through domain tools.
engineering_relevance: It can reduce manual trial-and-error in scientific software development by helping researchers rapidly discover, refine, and validate solution strategies for scorable physics and materials problems.
method: LLM-guided progressive local search over method design space
Uses domain-specific computational tools to maintain physical consistency
Stores reusable knowledge from both successful and failed exploration paths
evidence_signal: Across three benchmark tasks, PhyNex matched or exceeded human-designed state-of-the-art methods, with gains up to 3.8% in spectral similarity, 15.0% in normalized mean cut, and 5.9% in ergotropy at the 80k checkpoint.
watch_item: Performance depends on well-defined, quantitatively scorable objectives, so broader use may be limited for tasks with weak metrics or hard-to-encode physical constraints.
paper_title: Large Language Model Based Agent for Automated Discovery in Computational Physics
